g.text: All four leaf families set issuerRef: { name: kaalm-ca-issuer, kind: ClusterIssuer }. Neither the
gateway nor the controller ever reads the CA Secret: trust arrives only as the projected ConfigMap.
One Secret, one namespace, two readers: the CA ClusterIssuer
resolves ca.secretName only in cert-manager's --cluster-resource-namespace, and trust-manager reads
Bundle sources only from its --trust-namespace. Both default to cert-manager, so one copy serves both.
Put the Secret anywhere else (kaalm-system is the tempting mistake) and kaalm-ca-issuer goes
Ready=False, reason=SecretNotFound, and leaf issuance fails
Why a ClusterIssuer and not an Issuer:
a Certificate's issuerRef does not resolve a namespaced
Issuer across a namespace boundary. Only a ClusterIssuer lets the per-Agent and per-AgentTask
Certificates in user namespaces reference the same signing key.
